issue_title: Issue with phase transition for Account in local TZ
issue_body:
Consider the following scenario:

`TZ=America/New_York`, Ref time=`2023-03-01 03:47:56 UTC` (`2023-02-28 22:47:56 NY`) and BCD= 28.

Given a Plan with 2 Phases, a 3 months discount, then an evergreen, we expect the following:
* Invoice 1: 2023-02-28 -> 2023-03-28 (discount)
* Invoice 2: 2023-03-28 -> 2023-04-28 (discount)
* Invoice 3: 2023-04-28 -> 2023-05-28 (discount)
* Invoice 3: 2023-05-28 -> 2023-06-28 (evergreen)


However we observe a pro-ration after invoice 3 to re-align from `2023-05-28` -> `2023-06-01` because the computation of the phase effective date is incorrect (does not take into consideration the local TZ.

issue_title: Usage points at cancellation day not taken into account
issue_body:
In Kill Bill, by convention, the upper limit of an interval is always open. In practice, this means that when we bill from 2021-07-01 to 2021-08-01, we effectively bill for [2021-07-01, 2021-08-01), i.e. the last day being billed is 2021-07-31.

If a subscription starts at 3pm on 2021-07-01, we effectively bill for [2021-07-01T15:00:00, 2021-08-01T15:00:00), even though the invoice doesn't reflect the hour of the day.

When rolling up the raw usages, we are currently ignoring the last day, so any usages between [2021-08-01T00:00:00, 2021-08-01T15:00:00) are discarded (while these should be billed).

```
GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/1.0/kb/accounts/search/john?offset=0&limit=1&accountWithBalance=true&accountWithBalanceAndCBA=false
```

Results in:

```
org.glassfish.jersey.server.internal.process.MappableException: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: The template variable 'searchKey' has no value
```

It looks like we need to update `JaxrsUriBuilder` and pass the search key as such (instead of params):

```
uriBuilder.resolveTemplate("searchKey", "john")
```

This is likely a regression from https://github.com/killbill/killbill/issues/1384.

issue_title: Sub-millisecond precision in catalog date can lead to duplicate invoice items
issue_body: If the catalog effective date is set to `2020-09-16T10:34:25.348646Z` for instance, the DDL must support sub-millisecond precision (by default, it doesn't).

issue_title: Catalog filtering logic corrupts Catalog cache
issue_body:
The `clear()` method operates on the underlying cached catalog object:

https://github.com/killbill/killbill/blob/85017dde41a5591edfdffa13d2491c5eaf1828e6/jaxrs/src/main/java/org/killbill/billing/jaxrs/resources/JaxRsResourceBase.java#L822-L825

Reported on the mailing-list here: https://groups.google.com/forum/#!msg/killbilling-users/QssN_PfhNno/ILp8vAwkAgAJ
`isInitialized` is set during `startQueue` for the in-memory bus. It should be set in `initQueue` instead.

Aborted `payment_attempts` can have a corresponding row in `invoice_payments` with a `NULL` `payment_id` (note that the `payment_cookie_id` i.e. the `payment_attempts. transaction_external_key` won't be `NULL` though):

```
MySQL [killbill]> select * from invoice_payments order by record_id desc limit 1\G
*************************** 1. row ***************************
                record_id: 3
                       id: e7f6b1d0-3823-4c5b-9226-12327af64492
                     type: ATTEMPT
               invoice_id: 742a1a60-58f8-4ed2-b9db-b1816a741f44
               payment_id: NULL
             payment_date: 2012-05-02 00:37:59
                   amount: 0.000000000
                 currency: USD
       processed_currency: USD
        payment_cookie_id: bac67833-ac7c-458d-8e2a-659c0cdfb0b6
linked_invoice_payment_id: NULL
                  success: 0
               created_by: PaymentRequestProcessor
             created_date: 2012-05-02 00:37:59
        account_record_id: 1
         tenant_record_id: 0
1 row in set (0.00 sec)
```

This can trigger a NPE here: https://github.com/killbill/killbill/blob/1be24a4a144e80abcf45a5982deb7c1f2e22aa3b/jaxrs/src/main/java/org/killbill/billing/jaxrs/resources/InvoiceResource.java#L623

```
java.lang.NullPointerException: null
    at com.google.common.base.Preconditions.checkNotNull(Preconditions.java:877)
    at com.google.common.collect.ImmutableSet$Builder.add(ImmutableSet.java:483)
    at com.google.common.collect.ImmutableSet.copyOf(ImmutableSet.java:248)
    at com.google.common.collect.ImmutableSet.copyOf(ImmutableSet.java:230)
    at org.killbill.billing.jaxrs.resources.InvoiceResource.getPaymentsForInvoice(InvoiceResource.java:620)
```

Reported on the mailing-list: https://groups.google.com/forum/?#!msg/killbilling-users/Lv5EBreXeMY/Gx0DNTseDwAJ (note: overriding the BCD isn't necessary to reproduce the issue).

Related: https://github.com/killbill/killbill/issues/585

This can happen in the follow scenario:

1. Generate future invoice (assuming subscription has associated usage data)
2. Change plan (different `usageName`)
3. Regenerate invoice

Test scenario in branch [fix-for-1084](https://github.com/killbill/killbill/commit/1da394f2ac302bb88d57a3d5d1e852a9992a8955).

issue_title: Wrong computed invoice target date for non-UTC TZ
Direct buffer leak detection when using redis cache
issue_body:
The following bug was reported when configuring the system to use redis for caching:

```

	org.apache.coyote.AbstractProtocol$AbstractConnectionHandler.process(AbstractProtocol.java:625)
	org.apache.tomcat.util.net.AprEndpoint$SocketWithOptionsProcessor.run(AprEndpoint.java:2454)
	java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor.runWorker(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:1149)
	java.util.concurrent.ThreadPoolExecutor$Worker.run(ThreadPoolExecutor.java:624)
	org.apache.tomcat.util.threads.TaskThread$WrappingRunnable.run(TaskThread.java:61)
	java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:748)
```

issue_title: Janitor UNKNOWN transition may not correctly update invoice balance
issue_body:
The issue was reported on the [mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!searchin/killbilling-users/janitor%7Csort:date/killbilling-users/w1YNtBJ7Vxo/yf9GSTF7CAAJ).

By reading the code it looks like this may be a bug but this requires more investigation.
issue_comments: Also, what seems like another issue related to Janitor not making transition through state machines [mailing list thread](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/killbilling-users/Z4FEkZymPzk)

Regarding the second thread, I believe it is a duplicate of https://github.com/killbill/killbill/issues/880.

Regarding the original issue, it looks like it works if the Janitor is invoked via the GET APIs (`PaymentControlAwareRefresher#onJanitorChange` will call `IncompletePaymentAttemptTask#doIteration`), but not if the state if fixed directly by the notification queue (`IncompletePaymentTransactionTask`).

For this original issue, it looks like we should keep the `attemptId` -- if any in the notification -- and when processing the notification, if such  `attemptId` exists, recreate a new attempt, therefore calling the various control plugins.

I can confirm this behavior: when transitioning from `UNKNOWN` to `SUCCESS` the invoice balance is not updated. I managed to nail it down to this patch (against 0.20.10, needs to be rebased):
```
Index: payment/src/main/java/org/killbill/billing/payment/api/DefaultAdminPaymentApi.java
IDEA additional info:
Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP
<+>UTF-8
===================================================================
--- payment/src/main/java/org/killbill/billing/payment/api/DefaultAdminPaymentApi.java	(date 1554364168000)
+++ payment/src/main/java/org/killbill/billing/payment/api/DefaultAdminPaymentApi.java	(date 1562332274224)
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 
 package org.killbill.billing.payment.api;
 
+import java.math.BigDecimal;
 import java.util.List;
 
 import javax.annotation.Nullable;
@@ -116,7 +117,7 @@
                                                            lastSuccessPaymentState,
                                                            paymentTransaction.getId(),
                                                            transactionStatus,
-                                                           paymentTransaction.getProcessedAmount(),
+                                                           paymentSMHelper.isSuccessState(currentPaymentStateName) ? paymentTransaction.getAmount() : BigDecimal.ZERO,
                                                            paymentTransaction.getProcessedCurrency(),
                                                            paymentTransaction.getGatewayErrorCode(),
                                                            paymentTransaction.getGatewayErrorMsg(),

```
I was willing to make a PR, but I had no tests ready (nor I checked the existing ones :disappointed:), so I was quite sure the PR would have been rejected.

EDIT: at a new glance, maybe `BigDecimal.ZERO` could/should be replaced by `paymentTransaction.getProcessedAmount()`.

I've submitted https://github.com/killbill/killbill/pull/1216 to fix the original issue. The second report is tracked by #880.

@meden Your issue is actually unrelated to this bug. In your case, the invoice balance can indeed be incorrect either when calling the `DefaultAdminPaymentApi` via our JAX-RS endpoint or when calling this API from a poorly implemented payment plugin.

In any case, I've submitted a patch to fix this in https://github.com/killbill/killbill/pull/1216 -- could you verify it solves your use-case?

If it doesn't, please reach out to the mailing-list with details on how to reproduce it and I'll open a separate bug if the issue is still there. Thanks!

issue_title: Unable to change to a plan defined in a subsequent catalog version
issue_body:
Scenario triggering the issue:

1. Catalog effective at T0
2. Subscription created at T1
3. Update catalog at T1, adding a new plan
4. Change subscription to new plan

Step 4. fails as the catalog cannot find the new plan.

issue_title: NPE in DefaultInternalBillingApi (future cancellation with pending PHASE event)
issue_body:
Scenario:

* Base plan + add-on
* Future cancel the base plan when the add-on has a `PENDING` phase event
* Trigger a dry-run invoice and notice the following stacktrace:

```
java.lang.NullPointerException
at org.killbill.billing.junction.plumbing.billing.DefaultInternalBillingApi.getPlanPhaseSpecifierFromTransition(DefaultInternalBillingApi.java:243)
at org.killbill.billing.junction.plumbing.billing.DefaultInternalBillingApi.calculateBcdForTransition(DefaultInternalBillingApi.java:229)
at org.killbill.billing.junction.plumbing.billing.DefaultInternalBillingApi.addBillingEventsForSubscription(DefaultInternalBillingApi.java:213)
at org.killbill.billing.junction.plumbing.billing.DefaultInternalBillingApi.addBillingEventsForBundles(DefaultInternalBillingApi.java:168)
at org.killbill.billing.junction.plumbing.billing.DefaultInternalBillingApi.getBillingEventsForAccountAndUpdateAccountBCD(DefaultInternalBillingApi.java:109)
at org.killbill.billing.invoice.InvoiceDispatcher.processAccountWithLock(InvoiceDispatcher.java:279)
at org.killbill.billing.invoice.InvoiceDispatcher.processAccount(InvoiceDispatcher.java:250)
at org.killbill.billing.invoice.api.user.DefaultInvoiceUserApi.triggerInvoiceGeneration(DefaultInvoiceUserApi.java:215)
```

issue_title: Fix limitation where catalog plan name cannot end with an number
issue_body:
See the report from the [mailing list](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!searchin/killbilling-users/Failed$20to$20load$20overridden$20plan$20for$20tenant%7Csort:date/killbilling-users/m1HQ9ZSovDc/OFjeRtrQBQAJ).


The limitation come from the fact that the code interpret the catalog name string to understand whether such `Plan` is a plan with price override -- see code [here](https://github.com/killbill/killbill/blob/killbill-0.18.15/catalog/src/main/java/org/killbill/billing/catalog/caching/EhCacheOverriddenPlanCache.java#L88)

issue_title: Relax sanity checks for STANDALONE subscriptions
issue_body: https://github.com/killbill/killbill/blob/27fde0808eabca2801b9b3fed6a9cf4b3b58f7cf/entitlement/src/main/java/org/killbill/billing/entitlement/engine/core/DefaultEventsStream.java#L114 throws an NPE when trying to create the subscription.
issue_comments: This is unfortunately blocking our killbill evaluation.. is there any workaround? Or is the fix straightforward enough that perhaps I could submit a PR?

The fix is indeed straightforward (remove the `baseSubscription` `null` check) but we also need to:

* Add a regression test to catch this moving forward (i.e. ensure `DefaultEventsStream` builds fine for `STANDALONE` subscriptions).
* Add a test to verify that we are not missing a `null` check in `getPendingEntitlementCancellationEvents` for `STANDALONE` subscriptions (i.e. try the `uncancelEntitlement` API on a `STANDALONE` subscription).

If you would like to contribute, [TestDefaultEntitlement](https://github.com/killbill/killbill/blob/27fde0808eabca2801b9b3fed6a9cf4b3b58f7cf/entitlement/src/test/java/org/killbill/billing/entitlement/api/TestDefaultEntitlement.java) is probably a good starting point (lots of test examples).

issue_title: Invoice formatter display wrong currency symbol
issue_comments: We have a nice test to test that-- see testFormattedAmount in https://github.com/killbill/killbill/blob/master/invoice/src/test/java/com/ning/billing/invoice/template/formatters/TestDefaultInvoiceFormatter.java You can check that if you change to currency from EUR to USD, MXN, whatever you want, the result is the same and solely depend on the Locale.FRENCH that is passed. Supposing a Locale.FRANCE locale and a USD currency, do you want to keep the France number format but replacing the currency code by the invoice currency? ``` // Locale.FRANCE & Currency.EUR 1 499,95 € ``` ``` // Locale.FRANCE & Currency.USD 1 499,95 $ ``` > Supposing a Locale.FRANCE locale and a USD currency, do you want to keep the France number format but replacing the currency code by the invoice currency? Yes ([this](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/7828364/formatting-currencies-in-foreign-locales-in-java) might help). We should also add more tests to cover various use-cases (see examples [here](https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/goglobal/bb688126.aspx) and [here](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Number/toLocaleString)). Good. Yes, that's how I tried. It seems that when tries to use a different currency that the Locale has, the format prints the letters instead of the symbol. Example: // Locale.US & Currency.USD **$**1,499.95 // Locale.US & Currency.EUR **EUR**1,499.95 // Locale.FRANCE & Currency.EUR 1 499,95 **€** // Locale.FRANCE & Currency.USD 1 499,95 **USD** Is it the expected behaviour? > Is it the expected behaviour? It seems [that way](http://stackoverflow.com/questions/15106484/not-getting-currency-symbols-for-specific-locale), to avoid confusions (e.g. `$` can be ambiguous depending on the country).
